_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 7-22 Tourniquet Application (Revised 5/27/2023, MDHHS Reviewed 2023). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
protocol_reference: Michigan Procedures Protocol Section 7-22: Tourniquet Application — MDHHS Approved 5/27/2023
purpose: Tourniquet application for life-threatening extremity hemorrhage. Once hemorrhage is controlled, the tourniquet is NOT adjusted or removed in the prehospital setting. Notify receiving hospital of tourniquet application and time.

sections.title: Indications
points: Life-threatening extremity hemorrhage not controlled by direct pressure
Mass Casualty Incident (MCI): tourniquet for temporary hemorrhage control during triage and treatment

sections.title: Contraindications
points: NEVER apply over a joint (knee, elbow) — risk of peroneal or ulnar nerve damage; apply PROXIMAL to the joint
NEVER apply over an impaled object
Tourniquets are for EXTREMITY hemorrhage only — not applicable to truncal, neck, or junctional hemorrhage (use wound packing and hemostatic agents)

sections.title: Application Technique
points: Apply tourniquet directly to SKIN (not over clothing if possible) — proximal 2–3 inches from the wound
Tighten until arterial blood flow is occluded (no pulsatile bleeding distal to tourniquet)
Document time of application clearly on the tourniquet itself AND in the PCR
Perform neurovascular check distal to tourniquet every 5 minutes post-application
Notify receiving hospital of tourniquet presence, location, and time applied
Do NOT adjust or remove tourniquet once hemorrhage is controlled in the prehospital setting
If bleeding continues after first tourniquet: apply a second tourniquet ADJACENT (proximal) to the first — do not attempt to tighten the first

sections.title: Clinical Considerations
points: Risk of ischemic limb damage is minimal if total time from extrication to hospital arrival is less than 180 minutes (3 hours)
Contact Medical Control before considering loosening a tourniquet if clinical judgment raises questions about its appropriateness
Use commercial tourniquets (CAT, SOFT-T, etc.) when available — improvised tourniquets are less reliable
Cover tourniquet with bandage during transport but keep visible — do NOT conceal from receiving providers

deterioration_flags: Continued bleeding after first tourniquet — apply second tourniquet adjacent and proximal to first; do NOT re-tighten first
Tourniquet applied > 60 minutes — notify hospital; do not loosen prehospital without MC order
Signs of distal ischemia — monitor neurovascular status; do not remove without MC order; expedite transport
scope_notes: Apply to skin, proximal 2–3 inches from wound, not over joints or impaled objects
Tighten until arterial occlusion — no pulsatile bleeding
Document time on tourniquet AND in PCR
Do NOT adjust or remove once hemorrhage is controlled
Second tourniquet if first fails: apply adjacent proximal to first
Contact MC before loosening tourniquet prehospital
